title: Fragment: A Tale Untold.
content:
One sung of thee who left the tale untold,
    Like the false dawns which perish in the bursting;
    Like empty cups of wrought and daedal gold,
    Which mock the lips with air, when they are thirsting.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Percy Bysshe Shelley, titled "Fragment: A Tale Untold.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
